text: Make the current local project and .bowman root obvious in the UI
description: When Aimparency is connected to a project, the UI should clearly show which local repo/workspace is open and which `.bowman` directory is active. Users should not have to infer the target project from indirect state.

reflections.context: Make the active local project and .bowman root obvious in the connected UI, not just buried in settings.
reflections.outcome: The main app header now renders a dedicated project-location block with the project name, repo root, and .bowman path, and a focused test verifies it.
reflections.effectiveness: This was a small, direct UI-shell change that solved the trust/orientation problem without touching project loading flows.
reflections.lesson: For orientation problems, prefer a single always-visible surface in the app shell over duplicating detail in deeper modals.
reflections.pattern: This matches earlier Aimparency fixes where reducing hidden state and surfacing runtime context in the shell improved trust faster than adding more settings detail.

status.comment: App header now shows the active project name, repo root path, and derived .bowman root explicitly; added frontend regression coverage in App.spec.ts.
